things.name: Falkland Palace & Garden (NTS)
things.description: Royal Stuart hunting palace with Scotland's oldest real tennis court — once the playground of Mary Queen of Scots

things.name: Glenrothes Sculptures (Open-Air Art Trail)
things.description: Unique collection of over 100 outdoor sculptures distributed throughout the new town — one of the UK's largest free public art collections

things.name: Loch Leven Castle (Mary Queen of Scots)
things.description: Island castle on Loch Leven where Mary Queen of Scots was imprisoned for nearly a year before her dramatic 1568 escape by boat

things.name: Lomond Hills Regional Park
things.description: The East Lomond and West Lomond hills dominating the Fife skyline — wonderful walking with superb views across the Kingdom of Fife

things.name: Balgonie Castle
things.description: Privately owned and occupied 14th-century tower house — the oldest continuously inhabited castle in Fife with excellent guided tours

things.name: Scotland's Secret Bunker
things.description: Eerie Cold War nuclear command centre buried 30 metres underground — a self-contained city designed to house 300 people after nuclear attack

things.name: St Andrews (Day Trip)
things.description: The home of golf — the Old Course, medieval cathedral, castle and the oldest university in Scotland

things.name: Kirkcaldy Galleries (Day Trip)
things.description: Fife's finest art gallery with Scottish Colourists and local history collections — free entry and just 20 minutes from Glenrothes

things.name: Balbirnie Craft Centre & Park
things.description: Elegant Georgian estate parkland with an arts and crafts centre, gallery and a stone circle — a tranquil retreat near the town centre

things.name: Markinch Heritage Village
things.description: Pretty Fife village with a 12th-century Norman tower church, historic conservation area and the famous Balbirnie estate on its edge
